full_response:
Let the curve Γ be parameterised by the unprimed coordinate time t itself (any monotonic parameter would do).  
The coordinate transformation between the two Cartesian systems is  

  t′ = x sinh(a t) , x′ = x cosh(a t) , y′ = y , z′ = z .  (1)

Curve Γ in the unprimed system  
By definition Γ is the world-line along which the spatial coordinates are fixed at the constants (x₀ , y₀ , z₀).  
Hence, with t used as the parameter,

  (t, x, y, z)_Γ = ( t , x₀ , y₀ , z₀ ),   −∞ < t < ∞.  (2)

Curve Γ in the primed system  
Insert x = x₀ , y = y₀ , z = z₀ from (2) into the transformation (1):

  t′(t) = x₀ sinh(a t),  
  x′(t) = x₀ cosh(a t),  
  y′(t) = y₀,  
  z′(t) = z₀.  (3)

If one wishes to eliminate the parameter t, use cosh² – sinh² = 1:

  x′² – t′² = x₀²,  y′ = y₀,  z′ = z₀,  (4)

revealing that in the primed coordinates Γ is a branch of a hyperbola of constant radius x₀.

\boxed{ \;
\text{Unprimed coordinates of }\Gamma:\;(t,x,y,z)=(t,\;x_{0},\;y_{0},\;z_{0})\ ;
\qquad
\text{Primed coordinates of }\Gamma:\;(t',x',y',z')=(x_{0}\sinh(at),\;x_{0}\cosh(at),\;y_{0},\;z_{0})
\;}  

Confidence: 90%
